title: Probability, Counting, Expected Value, and Risk · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Compare warranties, games, reliability claims, and scheduling risks with transparent assumptions. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a practical risk and expected-value brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define outcomes and sample spaces before assigning probabilities.
Use complement, addition, and multiplication rules with explicit event relationships.
Interpret conditional probability as a changed reference group.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: An outcome pays $30 with probability 0.30 and $0 otherwise. Calculate its expected value. Use the result to decide Expected Value for a practical risk and expected-value brief.
steps.why: The Expected Value result for a practical risk and expected-value brief is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Weight each outcome by its probability: 30 × 0.30 + 0 × 0.70. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compute and interpret expected value without claiming it predicts a single outcome.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Expected Value, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a practical risk and expected-value brief. hints: Weight each outcome by its probability: 30 × 0.30 + 0 × 0.70.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.
